description: Canonical slice registry for the NixOS AI harness. Each slice declares owner paths, runtime surfaces, validation evidence, discoverability surfaces, and governance constraints.

observable.description: Validated plus health and operator-visible signals.

governed.description: Observable plus explicit risk, review, and rollback surfaces.

slices.name: Routing and Orchestration
slices.summary: Coordinator routing, model/profile selection, task classification, and orchestration policy.

invariants: profile names must resolve to declared routing surfaces
intent contracts must be preserved through workflow runs
routing decisions must expose enough information for validation and escalation

notes: This slice is the highest-leverage coordination layer and should default to governed maturity.

slices.name: Switchboard Front Door
slices.summary: OpenAI-compatible proxy that mediates profile-based local and remote model access.

invariants: declared profiles must remain internally consistent
default and continue-local behavior must stay stable for editor workflows

acceptance_signals: profile cards remain aligned with routing callers

notes: Inline Python in Nix remains a design debt called out by the routing architecture review.

slices.name: MCP Server Platform
slices.summary: Shared MCP server stack, tooling exposure, dispatch, and integration patterns across the harness.

invariants: tool contracts must be explicit
shared security middleware should be reused across servers
failure_modes: tool exposure without discoverability or audit

notes: This slice should eventually enforce a more uniform server manifest and middleware contract.

slices.name: AIDB, Memory, and Data
slices.summary: Knowledge ingestion, vector search, agent memory, and persistence surfaces for harness retrieval.

invariants: knowledge and memory stores must avoid secret leakage
stored context should carry enough provenance to be reviewed

acceptance_signals: document ingest and retrieval path remain healthy

notes: Provenance, trust, and retention should become stronger explicit fields over time.

slices.name: Workflow Engine
slices.summary: Blueprint-driven planning, persisted runs, phases, acceptance, and workflow execution support.

invariants: workflows must declare phases and exit criteria
runs must preserve intent contracts and reviewer visibility
failure_modes: execution without rollback or acceptance evidence

notes: Workflows are a core control-plane surface and should be treated as governed configuration.

slices.name: AQ Bootstrap and Hints Tooling
slices.summary: Onboarding, task bootstrap, context-card, and hint entrypoints for agents and operators.

invariants: bootstrap tools must remain compact and search-first
hint and context tools must route users to existing harness surfaces instead of hidden internals
failure_modes: context-card guidance diverges from workflow and harness behavior

acceptance_signals: hints and context bootstrap remain available

notes: This slice owns the initial harness entry experience and should remain compact and evidence-first.

slices.name: AQ Runtime Diagnostics Tooling
slices.summary: Runtime diagnosis, remediation planning, and QA phase runners for live service health and incident classification.

invariants: diagnostics should classify the failing layer before proposing changes
runtime actions should preserve reviewer-visible evidence and rollback paths
failure_modes: QA phase drift from runtime planner assumptions

acceptance_signals: runtime planner and diagnose entrypoints run successfully

notes: This slice should stay tightly aligned with live service health and avoid accumulating imperative-only remediation paths.

slices.name: AQ Workflow and Orchestration Tooling
slices.summary: CLI surfaces for workflow execution, orchestration entry, and capability-directed task routing.

invariants: workflow CLI behavior must stay aligned with blueprint contracts
orchestration tools should favor existing harness workflow surfaces over hidden custom flows
failure_modes: workflow CLI diverges from server-side workflow engine
task routing bypasses established workflow blueprints

acceptance_signals: workflow CLI validates and lists workflows
workflow orchestration remains reachable from CLI

notes: This slice should remain a thin operational layer over the workflow engine rather than a separate orchestration system.

slices.name: AQ Reporting and Knowledge Tooling
slices.summary: Reporting, memory lookup, retrieval prewarm, and pattern/gap tooling for operator feedback and knowledge reuse.

invariants: reporting and knowledge tooling should surface actionable operator guidance
memory and prewarm tools should reuse existing harness data stores instead of creating parallel persistence
failure_modes: memory surfaces become detached from AIDB and agent-memory ownership

acceptance_signals: memory CLI remains queryable and stats-capable

notes: This slice should eventually be cross-linked more tightly with the AIDB and memory data slice through shared acceptance signals.

slices.name: Monitoring and Observability
slices.summary: Metrics, alerts, dashboards, readiness views, and runtime health classification.

invariants: critical services must have health coverage
readiness claims should be backed by probeable evidence

acceptance_signals: metrics routes and readiness insights stay available

notes: This slice should become the operator-facing home for slice readiness over time.

slices.name: Dashboard and Operator Surfaces
slices.summary: Dashboard backend and frontend surfaces that make harness state visible and actionable for operators.

invariants: operator-facing routes should expose real system state, not only static claims
failure_modes: backend features hidden from the operator

acceptance_signals: dashboard insights routes return current repo-native readiness data

notes: This slice should eventually expose slice scorecard output directly.

slices.name: Agent Guidance and Communications
slices.summary: Prompts, hints, context cards, agent instruction sync, and workflow guidance surfaces.

invariants: guidance should remain compact, actionable, and repo-grounded
policy sync should not fork across agent surfaces
failure_modes: guidance not reachable through normal harness paths

notes: Prompt and context-card changes should eventually be tied to prompt evaluation evidence.

slices.name: Security and Governance
slices.summary: Repo-wide policy, secret handling, structure linting, validation gates, and governance enforcement for the harness.

invariants: secrets must not be hardcoded
repo structure policy must be enforced
validation must happen before commit and deploy
failure_modes: security checks that are documented but not enforced

notes: This is the meta-slice that should eventually consume scorecard output as one of its own readiness signals.
